174	def convert_dtype(ctx: "BytecodeContext", val: bc.Value,
175	                  fromty: Type, toty: Type) -> bc.Value:
176	    from_dtype = fromty.dtype if isinstance(fromty, TileTy) else fromty
177	    to_dtype = toty.dtype if isinstance(toty, TileTy) else toty
178	    toty_id = typeid(ctx.type_table, toty)
179	    if to_dtype == datatype.bool_ and datatype.is_integral(from_dtype):
180	        # TruncIOp is not doing pytorch style boolean casting (x != 0)
181	        # We have to use CmpIOp instead
182	        zero = ctx.constant(0, fromty)
183	        return bc.encode_CmpIOp(
184	            ctx.builder,
185	            result_type=toty_id,
186	            lhs=val,
187	            rhs=zero,
188	            comparison_predicate=bc.ComparisonPredicate.NOT_EQUAL,
189	            signedness=datatype.get_signedness(from_dtype))
190	    else:
191	        encoder = _get_type_conversion_encoder(from_dtype, to_dtype)
192	        return encoder(ctx.builder, toty_id, val)
193	
194	
195	def _broadcast_shape(ctx: "BytecodeContext",
196	                     val: bc.Value, fromty: TileTy, toty: TileTy):
197	    if len(fromty.shape) < len(toty.shape):
198	        # prepend 1s if input_shape have fewer dimensions
199	        diff = len(toty.shape) - len(fromty.shape)
200	        new_shape = (1,) * diff + fromty.shape
201	        reshaped_ty = TileTy(fromty.dtype, new_shape)
202	        reshaped_ty_id = typeid(ctx.type_table, reshaped_ty)
203	        val = bc.encode_ReshapeOp(ctx.builder, reshaped_ty_id, val)
204	        fromty = reshaped_ty
205	
206	    if fromty.shape != toty.shape:
207	        broadcasted_ty = TileTy(fromty.dtype, toty.shape)
208	        broadcasted_ty_id = typeid(ctx.type_table, broadcasted_ty)
209	        val = bc.encode_BroadcastOp(ctx.builder, broadcasted_ty_id, val)
210	        fromty = broadcasted_ty
211	    return val, fromty
212	
213	
214	def _get_reduce_indices(
215	    ctx: "BytecodeContext", input_shape: Tuple[int, ...], output_ty: TileTy,
216	    normalized_axis: int,
217	) -> bc.Value:
218	    tt = ctx.type_table
219	    # iota
220	    indices_ty = TileTy(
221	        output_ty.dtype, (input_shape[normalized_axis],)
222	    )
223	    indices = bc.encode_IotaOp(ctx.builder, typeid(tt, indices_ty))
224	
225	    # prepend and append 1 until normalized_axis is at the right dimension.
226	    new_shape = [1] * len(input_shape)
227	    new_shape[normalized_axis] = input_shape[normalized_axis]
228	    indices_ty = TileTy(
229	        output_ty.dtype, tuple(new_shape)
230	    )
231	    indices = bc.encode_ReshapeOp(ctx.builder, typeid(tt, indices_ty), indices)
232	    # broadcast to input_shape
233	    to_indices_ty = TileTy(output_ty.dtype, tuple(input_shape))
234	    res, _ = _broadcast_shape(ctx, indices, indices_ty, to_indices_ty)
235	    return res
236	
237	
238	def encode_comparison(builder: bc.CodeBuilder, fn: str, lhs: bc.Value, rhs: bc.Value,
239	                      dtype: DType, result_typeid: bc.TypeId) -> bc.Value:
240	    match fn:
241	        case "eq": pred = bc.ComparisonPredicate.EQUAL
242	        case "ne": pred = bc.ComparisonPredicate.NOT_EQUAL
243	        case "ge": pred = bc.ComparisonPredicate.GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL
244	        case "gt": pred = bc.ComparisonPredicate.GREATER_THAN
245	        case "le": pred = bc.ComparisonPredicate.LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL
246	        case "lt": pred = bc.ComparisonPredicate.LESS_THAN
247	
248	    if datatype.is_float(dtype):
249	        order = bc.ComparisonOrdering.UNORDERED if fn == 'ne' else bc.ComparisonOrdering.ORDERED
250	        return bc.encode_CmpFOp(builder,
251	                                result_type=result_typeid,
252	                                comparison_predicate=pred,
253	                                comparison_ordering=order,
254	                                lhs=lhs, rhs=rhs)
255	    elif datatype.is_integral(dtype) or datatype.is_boolean(dtype):
256	        return bc.encode_CmpIOp(builder,
257	                                result_type=result_typeid,
258	                                comparison_predicate=pred,
259	                                signedness=datatype.get_signedness(dtype),
260	                                lhs=lhs, rhs=rhs)
261	    else:
262	        raise TileInternalError(f'Unexpected dtype: {dtype}')
263	
264	
265	def create_synthetic_linkage_name(func_desc: FunctionDesc) -> str:
266	    # Build a synthetic linkage name for a helper function or lambda. Format:
267	    #
268	    #     <name>@<basename>:<line>:<column>_<specialization_id>
269	    #
270	    # By construction every FunctionDesc reaching this point has been
271	    # concretized by hir2ir — only the kernel entry skips this path (it uses
272	    # the externally-visible symbol instead), so the specialization_id is
273	    # required here.
274	    assert func_desc.specialization_id is not None, (
275	        f"create_synthetic_linkage_name called on a FunctionDesc without a "
276	        f"specialization_id: {func_desc}. hir2ir must concretize every "
277	        f"non-entry function before bytecode generation."
278	    )
279	    base_name = os.path.basename(func_desc.filename) or "unknown"
280	    stem = os.path.splitext(base_name)[0]
281	    # Convert any non-alphanumeric chars to _.
282	    stem = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_]", "_", stem) or "anonymous"
283	    func_part = func_desc.name if func_desc.name is not None else "lambda"
284	    return (f"{func_part}@{stem}:{func_desc.line}:{func_desc.column}"
285	            f"_{func_desc.specialization_id}")
286	
287	
288	class DebugAttrMap:
289	    def __init__(self,
290	                 debug_attr_table: bc.DebugAttrTable,
291	                 entry_symbol: str,
292	                 anonymize: bool):
293	        self._subprogram_cache = {}
294	        self._debug_attr_table = debug_attr_table
295	        self._entry_symbol = entry_symbol
296	        self._anonymize = anonymize
297	
298	    def _linkage_for(self, func_desc: FunctionDesc) -> str:
299	        # The kernel entry point keeps the externally-visible symbol so it can
300	        # be looked up by the loader. Every other function gets a per-function
301	        # artificial linkage name.
302	        if func_desc.is_entry:
303	            return self._entry_symbol
304	        return create_synthetic_linkage_name(func_desc)
305	
306	    def get_subprogram(self, func_desc: FunctionDesc) -> bc.DebugAttrId:
307	        # Every FunctionDesc reaching DI emission must satisfy: a function has
308	        # no specialization_id iff it is the kernel entry. hir2ir leaves the
309	        # entry's abstract desc as-is and concretizes everyone else; anything
310	        # else is a bug.
311	        assert func_desc.is_entry == (func_desc.specialization_id is None), (
312	            f"FunctionDesc invariant violated: is_entry={func_desc.is_entry} "
313	            f"but specialization_id={func_desc.specialization_id!r}: {func_desc}"
314	        )
315	        try:
316	            return self._subprogram_cache[func_desc]
317	        except KeyError:
318	            pass
319	
320	        func_dirname, func_basename = os.path.split(func_desc.filename)
321	        file_attr = self._debug_attr_table.file(func_basename, func_dirname)
322	        compile_unit_attr = self._debug_attr_table.compile_unit(file_attr)
323	        ret = self._debug_attr_table.subprogram(
324	            file=file_attr,
325	            line=func_desc.line,
326	            name="<lambda>" if func_desc.name is None else func_desc.name,
327	            linkage_name=self._linkage_for(func_desc),
328	            compile_unit=compile_unit_attr,
329	            scope_line=func_desc.line,
330	        )
331	        self._subprogram_cache[func_desc] = ret
332	        return ret
333	
334	    def get_debugattr(self, loc: Loc) -> bc.DebugAttrId:
335	        if self._anonymize or loc.is_unknown():
336	            return bc.MISSING_DEBUG_ATTR_ID
337	
338	        subprogram = self.get_subprogram(loc.function)
339	        attr = self._debug_attr_table.loc(subprogram, loc.filename, loc.line, loc.col)
340	        if loc.call_site is not None:
341	            caller_loc = self.get_debugattr(loc.call_site)
342	            attr = self._debug_attr_table.call_site(attr, caller_loc)
343	        return attr
344	
345	
346	class BytecodeContext:
347	    def __init__(self,
348	                 builder: bc.CodeBuilder,
349	                 type_table: bc.TypeTable,
350	                 debug_attr_map: DebugAttrMap,
351	                 global_section: bc.GlobalSection,
352	                 ir_ctx: IRContext,
353	                 sm_arch: str) -> None:
354	        self.builder = builder
355	        self.type_table = type_table
356	        self._debug_attr_map = debug_attr_map
357	        self.global_section = global_section
358	        self._typemap: Dict[str, Type] = ir_ctx.typemap
359	        self._constants: Dict[str, Any] = ir_ctx.constants
360	        self._value_map: Dict[str, bc.Value] = {}
361	        self._array_base_ptr: Dict[str, bc.Value] = {}
362	        self._list_partition_views: Dict[str, bc.Value] = {}
363	        self.sm_arch = sm_arch
364	        self.innermost_loop = None
365	
366	    @contextmanager
367	    def loc(self, loc: Loc):
368	        debug_attr_id = self._debug_attr_map.get_debugattr(loc)
369	        with loc, self.builder.debug_attr(debug_attr_id):
370	            yield
371	
372	    @contextmanager
373	    def enter_loop(self, loop):
374	        old = self.innermost_loop
375	        self.innermost_loop = loop
376	        try:
377	            yield
378	        finally:
379	            self.innermost_loop = old
380	
381	    def typeof(self, var: Var) -> Type:
382	        return self._typemap[var.name]
383	
384	    def typeid_of(self, var: Var) -> bc.TypeId:
385	        return typeid(self.type_table, self.typeof(var))
386	
387	    def is_constant(self, var: Var) -> bool:
388	        return var.name in self._constants
389	
390	    def get_constant(self, var: Var):
391	        return self._constants[var.name]
392	
393	    def get_constant_or_default(self, var: Var, default=None):
394	        return self._constants.get(var.name, default)
395	
396	    def get_value(self, var: Var) -> bc.Value:
397	        return self._value_map[var.name]
398	
399	    def get_optional_value(self, var: Var) -> Optional[bc.Value]:
400	        if var.name in self._constants and self._constants[var.name] is None:
401	            return None
402	        else:
403	            return self.get_value(var)
404	
405	    def set_value(self, var: Var, value: bc.Value) -> None:
406	        name = var.name
407	        if name in self._value_map:
408	            raise ValueError(f"Variable {name} is already in the value map")
409	        self._value_map[name] = value
410	
411	    def cast(self, val: bc.Value, fromty: Type, toty: Type) -> bc.Value:
412	        assert isinstance(fromty, TileTy)
413	        assert isinstance(toty, TileTy)
414	        if fromty == toty:
415	            return val
416	        if fromty.shape != toty.shape:
417	            val, fromty = _broadcast_shape(self, val, fromty, toty)
418	        if fromty.dtype != toty.dtype:
419	            val = convert_dtype(self, val, fromty, toty)
420	        return val
421	
422	    def bitcast(self, value: bc.Value, fromty: Type, toty: Type) -> bc.Value:
423	        assert isinstance(fromty, TileTy)
424	        assert isinstance(toty, TileTy)
425	        if fromty == toty:
426	            return value
427	        if fromty.shape != toty.shape:
428	            value, fromty = _broadcast_shape(self, value, fromty, toty)
429	        if fromty.dtype != toty.dtype:
430	            value = bc.encode_BitcastOp(self.builder, typeid(self.type_table, toty), value)
431	        return value
432	
433	    def constant(self, value, ty: Type) -> bc.Value:
434	        if not isinstance(ty, TileTy):
435	            raise TypeError(f"Cannot encode a constant of type {ty}; expected a TileTy")
436	
437	        def get_numel(v):
438	            if not isinstance(v, tuple):
439	                return 1
440	            return sum(get_numel(i) for i in v)
441	
442	        if get_numel(value) == 1:
443	            while isinstance(value, tuple):
444	                value = value[0]
445	            data = _constant_to_bytes(value, ty.dtype)
446	        else:
447	            assert isinstance(value, tuple)
448	            data = _constant_tuple_to_bytes(value, ty.dtype, ty.shape)
449	        return bc.encode_ConstantOp(self.builder, typeid(self.type_table, ty), data)
450	
451	    def index_tuple(self,
452	                    index: tuple[Var, ...], *, keep_i64: bool = False) -> Tuple[bc.Value, ...]:
453	        i32_tile_ty = self.type_table.tile(self.type_table.I32, ())
454	        item_types = tuple(x.get_type() for x in index)
455	        index_values = tuple(self.get_value(x) for x in index)
456	        if keep_i64:
457	            return index_values
458	        return tuple(
459	            bc.encode_TruncIOp(self.builder, i32_tile_ty, v, bc.IntegerOverflow.NONE)
460	            if (t.dtype if isinstance(t, TileTy) else t).bitwidth > 32 else v
461	            for v, t in zip(index_values, item_types, strict=True)
462	        )
463	
464	    def load_store_hints(self,
465	                         latency: Optional[int],
466	                         allow_tma: Optional[bool]) -> Optional[bc.OptimizationHints]:
467	        if latency is None and allow_tma is None:
468	            return None
469	        if allow_tma is None:
470	            allow_tma = True
471	        load_store_hints = bc.LoadStoreHints(latency=latency, allow_tma=allow_tma)
472	        if self.builder.version < BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
473	            return make_load_store_hints({self.sm_arch: load_store_hints})
474	        else:
475	            return make_load_store_hints({"default": load_store_hints})
476	
477	
478	def generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx: BytecodeContext, block: Block):
479	    for op in block.operations:
480	        with ctx.loc(op.loc):
481	            try:
482	                result_values = op.generate_bytecode(ctx)
483	                if isinstance(result_values, bc.Value):
484	                    result_values = (result_values,)
485	
486	                for result_var, val in zip(op.result_vars, result_values, strict=True):
487	                    assert isinstance(val, bc.Value)
488	                    ctx.set_value(result_var, val)
489	            except TileError:
490	                raise
491	            except Exception as e:
492	                raise TileInternalError(f"Internal error: {e}") from e
493	
494	
495	def _resolve_num_worker_warps(num_worker_warps: Optional[int],
496	                              version: BytecodeVersion) -> Optional[int]:
497	    if num_worker_warps is not None and version < BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
498	        warnings.warn(
499	            f"num_worker_warps is ignored: requires tileiras {BytecodeVersion.V_13_3.as_string()},"
500	            f" but current version is {version.as_string()}."
501	        )
502	        return None
503	
504	    return num_worker_warps
505	
506	
507	def generate_bytecode_for_kernel(func_body: Block,
508	                                 symbol: str,
509	                                 compiler_options: CompilerOptions,
510	                                 sm_arch: str,
511	                                 writer: bc.BytecodeWriter,
512	                                 anonymize_debug_attr: bool):
513	    version = writer.version
514	    hints_by_target = compiler_options.hints_by_target()
515	    if version < BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
516	        specialized_hints = dict(hints_by_target.get("default", {}))
517	        specialized_hints.update(hints_by_target.get(sm_arch, {}))
518	        hints_by_target = {sm_arch: specialized_hints}
519	
520	    hints = {
521	        target: bc.EntryHints(
522	            num_cta_in_cga=fields.get("num_ctas"),
523	            occupancy=fields.get("occupancy"),
524	            num_worker_warps_per_cta=_resolve_num_worker_warps(fields.get("num_worker_warps"),
525	                                                               version))
526	        for target, fields in hints_by_target.items()
527	    }
528	
529	    param_type_ids = [typeid(writer.type_table, p.get_type()) for p in func_body.params]
530	    debug_attr_map = DebugAttrMap(writer.debug_attr_table, symbol,
531	                                  anonymize=anonymize_debug_attr)
532	    func_debug_attr = debug_attr_map.get_debugattr(func_body.loc)
533	
534	    with writer.function(name=symbol,
535	                         parameter_types=param_type_ids,
536	                         result_types=(),
537	                         entry_point=True,
538	                         hints=hints,
539	                         debug_attr=func_debug_attr) as (builder, param_values):
540	        ctx = BytecodeContext(builder=builder,
541	                              type_table=writer.type_table,
542	                              debug_attr_map=debug_attr_map,
543	                              global_section=writer.global_section,
544	                              ir_ctx=func_body.ctx,
545	                              sm_arch=sm_arch)
546	
547	        for var, value in zip(func_body.params, param_values, strict=True):
548	            ctx.set_value(var, value)
549	
550	        generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx, func_body)
551

6	from dataclasses import dataclass
7	from typing import Optional, List, Sequence, Tuple

10	from .basic import encode_varint, encode_int_list, StringTable

17	@dataclass
18	class Value:
19	    value_id: int
20

22	class NestedBlockBuilder:
23	    def __init__(self,
24	                 code_builder: "CodeBuilder",
25	                 num_results: int,
26	                 num_blocks: int):
27	        self._code_builder = code_builder
28	        self._num_results = num_results
29	        self._num_blocks = num_blocks
30	
31	    @contextmanager
32	    def new_block(self, arg_type_ids: Sequence[TypeId]):
33	        assert self._num_blocks > 0
34	        self._code_builder.buf.append(1)  # number of blocks in region (always 1)
35	        encode_varint(len(arg_type_ids), self._code_builder.buf)
36	        for t in arg_type_ids:
37	            encode_typeid(t, self._code_builder.buf)
38	        orig_buf = self._code_builder.buf
39	        orig_next_value_id = self._code_builder.next_value_id
40	        orig_num_ops = self._code_builder.num_ops
41	        self._code_builder.num_ops = 0
42	        self._code_builder.buf = bytearray()
43	        try:
44	            yield self._code_builder._make_value_tuple(len(arg_type_ids))
45	            encode_varint(self._code_builder.num_ops, orig_buf)
46	            orig_buf.extend(self._code_builder.buf)
47	            self._num_blocks -= 1
48	        finally:
49	            self._code_builder.next_value_id = orig_next_value_id
50	            self._code_builder.num_ops = orig_num_ops
51	            self._code_builder.buf = orig_buf
52	
53	    def done(self) -> Tuple[Value, ...]:
54	        assert self._num_blocks == 0
55	        return self._code_builder._make_value_tuple(self._num_results)
56	
57	
58	@dataclass
59	class CodeBuilder:
60	    buf: bytearray
61	    version: BytecodeVersion
62	    string_table: StringTable
63	    constant_table: ConstantTable
64	    debug_attr_per_op: List[DebugAttrId]
65	    next_value_id: int = 0
66	    cur_debug_attr: DebugAttrId = DebugAttrId(0)
67	    num_ops: int = 0
68	
69	    def new_op(self, num_results: Optional[int] = None) -> Value | Tuple[Value, ...] | None:
70	        self.debug_attr_per_op.append(self.cur_debug_attr)
71	        self.num_ops += 1
72	        if num_results is None:
73	            ret = Value(self.next_value_id)
74	            self.next_value_id += 1
75	            return ret
76	        elif num_results == 0:
77	            return None
78	        else:
79	            return self._make_value_tuple(num_results)
80	
81	    def new_op_with_nested_blocks(self,
82	                                  num_results: int,
83	                                  num_blocks: int) -> NestedBlockBuilder:
84	        self.debug_attr_per_op.append(self.cur_debug_attr)
85	        self.num_ops += 1
86	        encode_varint(num_blocks, self.buf)
87	        return NestedBlockBuilder(self, num_results=num_results, num_blocks=num_blocks)
88	
89	    def _make_value_tuple(self, length: int) -> Tuple[Value, ...]:
90	        end = self.next_value_id + length
91	        ret = tuple(Value(i) for i in range(self.next_value_id, end))
92	        self.next_value_id = end
93	        return ret
94	
95	    @contextmanager
96	    def debug_attr(self, debug_attr_id: DebugAttrId):
97	        old = self.cur_debug_attr
98	        self.cur_debug_attr = debug_attr_id
99	        try:
100	            yield
101	        finally:
102	            self.cur_debug_attr = old
103	
104	    def encode_opattr_bool(self, val: bool):
105	        self.buf.append(bool(val))
106	
107	    def encode_opattr_int(self, val: int):
108	        encode_varint(val, self.buf)
109	
110	    def encode_opattr_enum(self, expected_type, val):
111	        assert isinstance(val, expected_type)
112	        self.buf.extend(val._value_)
113	
114	    def encode_opattr_str(self, val: str):
115	        encode_varint(self.string_table[val.encode()].string_id, self.buf)
116	
117	    def encode_opattr_typeid(self, type_id: TypeId):
118	        encode_typeid(type_id, self.buf)
119	
120	    def encode_opattr_array(self, arr: Sequence[TaggedAttribute]):
121	        encode_varint(len(arr), self.buf)
122	        for x in arr:
123	            self.encode_opattr_tagged(TaggedAttribute, x)
124	
125	    def encode_opattr_tagged(self, attr_class, val):
126	        assert isinstance(val, attr_class)
127	        val.encode_tagged(self.string_table, self.buf)
128	
129	    def encode_opattr_dense_typed_elements(self, val: bytes):
130	        cid = self.constant_table.dense_constant(val)
131	        encode_varint(cid.constant_id, self.buf)
132	
133	    def encode_opattr_dense_int32_array(self, val: Sequence[int]):
134	        encode_int_list(val, 4, self.buf)
135	
136	    def encode_opattr_dense_bool_array(self, val: Sequence[bool]):
137	        encode_int_list([int(bool(x)) for x in val], 1, self.buf)
138	
139	    def encode_opattr_optimization_hints(self, val: OptimizationHints):
140	        encode_dictionary_untagged(val.hints_by_arch, self.string_table, self.buf)
141	
142	
143	def encode_optional_operand(val: Optional[Value], buf: bytearray):
144	    if val is not None:
145	        encode_varint(val.value_id, buf)
146	
147	
148	def encode_unsized_variadic_operands(vals: Sequence[Value], buf: bytearray):
149	    for v in vals:
150	        encode_varint(v.value_id, buf)
151	
152	
153	def encode_sized_variadic_operands(vals: Sequence[Value], buf: bytearray):
154	    encode_varint(len(vals), buf)

532	            kind = f.metadata.get(_FIELD_METADATA_KEY) if isinstance(f, dataclasses.Field) else None
533	            if kind == _FieldKind.OPERAND:
534	                operand_names.append(field_name)

536	                attribute_names.append(field_name)

541	                                f" operand(), attribute() or nested_block()")

554	                # Don't allow aggregate values as operands, except for arrays and lists.
555	                # All other aggregates should only exist in the HIR level.

568	    def _clone_impl(self, mapper: Mapper, result_vars: Sequence[Var]) -> Operation:
569	        new_fields = {}
570	
571	        for name in self._attribute_names:
572	            new_fields[name] = getattr(self, name)
573	
574	        for name in self._operand_names:
575	            var = getattr(self, name)
576	            if isinstance(var, Var):
577	                new_var = mapper.get_var(var)
578	            elif var is None:
579	                new_var = None
580	            else:
581	                new_var = tuple(mapper.get_var(v) for v in var)
582	            new_fields[name] = new_var
583	
584	        for name in self._nested_block_names:
585	            old_block = getattr(self, name)
586	            new_block = Block(old_block.ctx, old_block.loc)
587	            new_block.params = mapper.clone_vars(old_block.params)
588	            for old_op in old_block:
589	                new_block.append(old_op.clone(mapper))
590	            new_fields[name] = new_block
591	
592	        return type(self)(result_vars=tuple(result_vars), loc=self.loc, **new_fields)

59	        result_type_ids = [typeid(ctx.type_table, ty) for ty in types]

64	                                             initial_values, unsignedCmp=False)
65	            induction_var_type_id = ctx.typeid_of(self.induction_var)
66	            block_arg_type_ids = (induction_var_type_id, *result_type_ids)
67	        else:

69	            block_arg_type_ids = result_type_ids
70

72	            block_args = iter(block_args)
73	            if self.is_for_loop:
74	                ctx.set_value(self.induction_var, next(block_args))
75	            for var, value in zip(self.body_vars, block_args, strict=True):
76	                ctx.set_value(var, value)

107	async def loop_impl(body: hir.Block, iterable: Var):
108	    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block, retarget_loc
109	
110	    scope = Scope.get_current()
111	    range_ty = require_optional_range_type(iterable)
112	    if range_ty is None and body.jump == hir.Jump.BREAK and not _have_nested_jump(body.calls):
113	        # In ast2hir, we create a loop around the function body in order to support early returns.
114	        # But if there is no early return, we can remove the loop. In this case, the loop will only
115	        # have a "break" at the end of the loop body, and no other break/continue statements.
116	        info = ControlFlowInfo((), flatten=True)
117	        with scope.change_loop_info(info):
118	            await dispatch_hir_block(body)
119	        return
120	
121	    builder = Builder.get_current()
122	    stored_locals = tuple(sorted(body.stored_indices))
123	    var_states = tuple(LoopVarState(PhiState(initial_constant_state=ConstantState.NONCONSTANT),
124	                                    PhiState())
125	                       for _ in stored_locals)
126	    initial_values = tuple(scope.local.get(index, builder.loc) for index in stored_locals)
127	    body_params = []
128	
129	    # Logic specific to `for` loops:
130	    if range_ty is not None:
131	        # A `for` loop may have 0 iterations, so initial values need to be propagated
132	        # to the loop's results.
133	        for initial_var, state in zip(initial_values, var_states, strict=True):
134	            state.result_phi.propagate(initial_var)
135	        # Create an induction variable
136	        induction_var = builder.ir_ctx.make_temp(builder.loc)
137	        induction_var.set_type(builder.ir_ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(range_ty.dtype, ()))
138	        scope.hir2ir_varmap[body.params[0].id] = induction_var
139	        body_params.append(induction_var)
140	
141	    # Process the loop body
142	    loop_info = ControlFlowInfo(stored_locals)
143	    body_loc = retarget_loc(body.loc, scope)
144	    with enter_nested_block(body_loc) as new_body, scope.change_loop_info(loop_info), \
145	            scope.local.enter_branch():
146	        # Define body variables. Not all of them will eventually be kept,
147	        # so we don't set the block parameters yet.
148	        body_vars = []
149	        for local_idx, initial_var, state in zip(
150	                stored_locals, initial_values, var_states, strict=True):
151	            state.body_phi.propagate(initial_var, allow_loose_typing=False)
152	            body_var = scope.local.redefine(local_idx, state.body_phi.last_loc)
153	            body_var.set_type(state.body_phi.ty)
154	            body_vars.append(body_var)
155	
156	        flat_body_vars = flatten_block_parameters(body_vars)
157	
158	        # Dispatch the body (hir.Block) to populate the new_body (ir.Block) with Operations
159	        await dispatch_hir_block(body)

161	    # Propagate type information from Continue/Break to body/result phis
162	    for jump_info in loop_info.jumps:
163	        is_continue = isinstance(jump_info.jump_op, Continue)
164	        assert is_continue or isinstance(jump_info.jump_op, Break)
165	        for output, state in zip(jump_info.outputs, var_states, strict=True):
166	            if is_continue:
167	                state.body_phi.propagate(output, fail_eagerly=True)
168	            if range_ty is not None or not is_continue:
169	                state.result_phi.propagate(output)

171	    # Determine the final loop variable types and filter out invalid variables
172	    mask = []
173	    for i, (body_var, state) in enumerate(zip(body_vars, var_states, strict=True)):
174	        was_valid = not isinstance(body_var.get_type_allow_invalid(), InvalidType)
175	        state.finalize_loopvar_type(body_var)
176	        ty = body_var.get_type_allow_invalid()
177	        is_valid = not isinstance(ty, InvalidType)
178	        mask.append(is_valid)
179	        if not was_valid and is_valid and ty.is_aggregate():
180	            # The initial variable is invalid but the loop variable is preserved,
181	            # and the loop variable is aggregate. In this case, `flat_body_vars[i]`
182	            # will contain a single variable (previously of InvalidType,
183	            # and now of an aggregate type). Thus, we need to update it with
184	            # the according number of flattened undefined variables.
185	            assert len(flat_body_vars[i]) == 1
186	            undefined_items = expand_aggregate_var(body_var)
187	            flat_body_vars[i] = undefined_items
188	            # Create a fake aggregate value so that flatten_aggregates() doesn't fail
189	            # when we update the Continue/Break statements later.
190	            body_var.set_aggregate(TupleValue(undefined_items))
191	
192	    # Set the block's parameters
193	    all_flattened_body_vars = sum((flattened for flattened, is_valid
194	                                   in zip(flat_body_vars, mask, strict=True) if is_valid), ())
195	    body_params.extend(all_flattened_body_vars)
196	    new_body.params = tuple(body_params)
197	    valid_var_types = tuple(v.get_type() for v, is_valid in zip(body_vars, mask, strict=True)
198	                            if is_valid)
199	    flat_var_types = flatten_aggregate_types(valid_var_types)
200	
201	    # Update Continue/Break statements
202	    for jump_info in loop_info.jumps:
203	        values = tuple(out
204	                       for out, is_valid in zip(jump_info.outputs, mask, strict=True)
205	                       if is_valid)
206	        flat_values = flatten_aggregates(values, valid_var_types)
207	        # For undefined break/continue value, add a MakeDummy op as its producer
208	        flat_values = _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(flat_values, flat_var_types)
209	        assert len(flat_values) == len(all_flattened_body_vars)
210	        jump_info.jump_op.values = flat_values
211	
212	    # Create the loop Operation
213	    valid_initial_values = tuple(v for v, is_valid
214	                                 in zip(initial_values, mask, strict=True)
215	                                 if is_valid)
216	    flat_initial_values = flatten_aggregates(valid_initial_values, valid_var_types)
217	    # For undefined initial value, add a MakeDummy op as its producer
218	    flat_initial_values = _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(flat_initial_values, flat_var_types)
219	    assert len(flat_initial_values) == len(all_flattened_body_vars)
220	
221	    if range_ty is None:
222	        start = stop = step = None
223	    else:
224	        range_val = iterable.get_aggregate()
225	        assert isinstance(range_val, RangeValue)
226	        start, stop, step = range_val.start, range_val.stop, range_val.step
227	    flat_result_vars = add_operation_variadic(Loop, flat_var_types,
228	                                              start=start,
229	                                              stop=stop,
230	                                              step=step,
231	                                              initial_values=flat_initial_values,
232	                                              body=new_body)
233	
234	    result_types = tuple(s.result_phi.ty for s, is_valid in zip(var_states, mask, strict=True)
235	                         if is_valid)
236	    result_vars = unflatten_aggregates(flat_result_vars, valid_var_types, result_types)
237	
238	    # Finalize the scope & type information for valid result variables
239	    valid_var_states = tuple(s for s, is_valid in zip(var_states, mask, strict=True)
240	                             if is_valid)
241	    valid_stored_locals = tuple(local_idx
242	                                for local_idx, is_valid in zip(stored_locals, mask, strict=True)
243	                                if is_valid)
244	    for res, state, local_idx in zip(result_vars, valid_var_states, valid_stored_locals,
245	                                     strict=True):
246	        state.result_phi.finalize_constant_and_loose_type(res)
247	        store_var(local_idx, res, state.result_phi.last_loc)
248	
249	    # For any names that are stored within the loop body but have an invalid result type,
250	    # we update the scope to point to an undefined variable of this invalid type, so that
251	    # using that variable afterwards would result in a type error.
252	    for body_var, state, local_idx, is_valid in zip(body_vars, var_states, stored_locals, mask,
253	                                                    strict=True):
254	        if not is_valid:
255	            store_invalid(local_idx, body_var.get_type_allow_invalid(),
256	                          state.result_phi.last_loc)
257	
258	    # Do this check at the end because this may be an automatically inserted loop
259	    # around the helper function's body.
260	    if builder.block_restriction is not None:
261	        builder.block_restriction.validate_operation(Loop)
262	
263	
264	def _have_nested_jump(calls: Sequence[hir.Call]) -> bool:
265	    return any(
266	        block.jump != hir.Jump.END_BRANCH or _have_nested_jump(block.calls)
267	        for c in calls
268	        if c.callee is hir_stubs.if_else
269	        for block in c.args[1:]
270	    )
271	
272	
273	@dataclass(eq=False)
274	class IfElse(Operation, opcode="ifelse"):
275	    cond: Var = operand()
276	    then_block: Block = nested_block()
277	    else_block: Block = nested_block()
278	
279	    @override
280	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[bc.Value, ...]:
281	        cond_val = ctx.get_value(self.cond)
282	        result_types = tuple(ctx.typeof(v) for v in self.result_vars)
283	        result_type_ids = tuple(typeid(ctx.type_table, t) for t in result_types)
284	        nested_builder = bc.encode_IfOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, cond_val)
285	
286	        for block in (self.then_block, self.else_block):
287	            with nested_builder.new_block(()):
288	                generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx, block)
289	
290	        return nested_builder.done()
291	
292	    @override
293	    def _to_string_block_prefixes(self) -> list[str]:
294	        return ["then", "else"]
295	
296	    @override
297	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
298	        return f"if(cond={self.cond})"
299	
300	
301	@impl(hir_stubs.if_else)
302	async def if_else_impl(cond: Var, then_block: hir.Block, else_block: hir.Block) -> Var | None:
303	    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block, retarget_loc
304	
305	    require_bool_scalar_type(cond)
306	    if cond.is_constant():
307	        branch_taken = then_block if cond.get_constant() else else_block
308	        return await _flatten_branch(branch_taken)
309	
310	    builder = Builder.get_current()
311	    if builder.block_restriction is not None:
312	        builder.block_restriction.validate_operation(IfElse)
313	
314	    # Get the total number of results by adding the number of stored variables.
315	    # Note: we sort the stored variable indices to make the order deterministic.
316	    scope = Scope.get_current()
317	    stored_locals = tuple(sorted(then_block.stored_indices | else_block.stored_indices))
318	
319	    # Convert the "then" branch from HIR to IR
320	    info = ControlFlowInfo(stored_locals)
321	    then_loc = retarget_loc(then_block.loc, scope)
322	    with enter_nested_block(then_loc) as new_then_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
323	            scope.local.enter_branch():
324	        await dispatch_hir_block(then_block)
325	
326	    # If "then" branch doesn't yield, transform our if-else into the following:
327	    #    if cond:
328	    #        <then_block>
329	    #    else:
330	    #        EndBranch
331	    #    <else_block>
332	    # This is to avoid the situation where none of the branches yield.
333	    else_loc = retarget_loc(else_block.loc, scope)
334	    if len(info.jumps) == 0:
335	        info = ControlFlowInfo(())
336	        with enter_nested_block(else_loc) as new_else_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
337	                scope.local.enter_branch():
338	            end_branch(None)
339	        add_operation_variadic(IfElse, (),
340	                               cond=cond, then_block=new_then_block, else_block=new_else_block)
341	        return await _flatten_branch(else_block)
342	
343	    # Convert the "else" branch from HIR to IR
344	    with enter_nested_block(else_loc) as new_else_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
345	            scope.local.enter_branch():
346	        await dispatch_hir_block(else_block)
347	
348	    # Do type/constant propagation
349	    num_results = len(info.jumps[0].outputs)
350	    result_phis = tuple(PhiState() for _ in range(num_results))
351	    for jump_info in info.jumps:
352	        for phi, v in zip(result_phis, jump_info.outputs, strict=True):
353	            phi.propagate(v)
354	
355	    # Determine which results have valid types
356	    mask = tuple(not isinstance(phi.ty, InvalidType) for phi in result_phis)
357	    valid_result_types = tuple(phi.ty for phi, is_valid in zip(result_phis, mask) if is_valid)
358	
359	    # Update the EndBranch operations by setting the outputs
360	    for jump_info in info.jumps:
361	        outputs = tuple(v for v, is_valid in zip(jump_info.outputs, mask, strict=True)
362	                        if is_valid)
363	        flat_outputs = flatten_aggregates(outputs, valid_result_types)
364	        jump_info.jump_op.outputs = flat_outputs
365	
366	    # Generate an IfElse op
367	    flat_types = flatten_aggregate_types(valid_result_types)
368	    flat_results = add_operation_variadic(IfElse, flat_types, cond=cond,
369	                                          then_block=new_then_block, else_block=new_else_block)
370	    valid_results = unflatten_aggregates(flat_results, valid_result_types, valid_result_types)
371	
372	    # Finalize the constant/loose type information
373	    valid_result_phis = tuple(phi for phi, is_valid in zip(result_phis, mask) if is_valid)
374	    for var, phi in zip(valid_results, valid_result_phis, strict=True):
375	        phi.finalize_constant_and_loose_type(var)
376	
377	    it = iter(valid_results)
378	    all_results = tuple(next(it) if is_valid else None for is_valid in mask)
379	    assert next(it, None) is None
380	
381	    # Get/create variables for the explicit result
382	    num_explicit_results = num_results - len(stored_locals)
383	    if num_explicit_results == 0:
384	        ret = None
385	    else:
386	        assert num_explicit_results == 1
387	        ret = all_results[0]
388	        if ret is None:
389	            assert isinstance(result_phis[0].ty, InvalidType)
390	            ret = builder.ir_ctx.make_temp(builder.loc)
391	            ret.set_type(result_phis[0].ty)
392	
393	    # Update the scope for stored named
394	    for res_var, phi, local_idx in zip(all_results[num_explicit_results:],
395	                                       result_phis[num_explicit_results:],
396	                                       stored_locals, strict=True):
397	        if res_var is None:
398	            store_invalid(local_idx, phi.ty, phi.last_loc)
399	        else:
400	            store_var(local_idx, res_var, phi.last_loc)
401	
402	    return ret
403	
404	
405	@impl(hir_stubs.tuple_comp_if)
406	async def tuple_comp_if_impl(cond: Var, then_block: hir.Block) -> None:
407	    require_bool_scalar_type(cond)
408	    if not cond.is_constant():
409	        raise TileTypeError("Tuple comprehension if-conditions must be statically known;"
410	                            " if the condition can be evaluated at compile time,"
411	                            " wrap it with ct.static_eval()")
412	    if cond.get_constant():
413	        await _flatten_branch(then_block)
414	
415	
416	async def _flatten_branch(branch: hir.Block) -> Var | None:
417	    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block
418	    info = ControlFlowInfo((), flatten=True)
419	    with Scope.get_current().change_if_else_info(info):
420	        await dispatch_hir_block(branch)
421	    if len(info.jumps) == 0:
422	        return None
423	    else:
424	        assert len(info.jumps) == 1
425	        jump = info.jumps[0]
426	        assert len(jump.outputs) in (0, 1)
427	        return None if len(jump.outputs) == 0 else jump.outputs[0]
428	
429	
430	# Maps to ContinueOp in TileIR
431	@dataclass(eq=False)
432	class Continue(Operation, opcode="continue", terminator=True):
433	    values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
434	
435	    @override
436	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
437	        next_values = [ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.values]
438	        bc.encode_ContinueOp(ctx.builder, next_values)
439	        return ()
440	
441	    @override
442	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
443	        return f"continue {', '.join([x.name for x in self.values])}"
444	
445	
446	def continue_():
447	    scope = Scope.get_current()
448	    info = scope.loop_info
449	    assert info is not None
450	    assert not info.flatten
451	
452	    for ctx_state in scope.context_stack[scope.loop_context_stack_depth:]:
453	        ctx_state.exit_callback()
454	
455	    builder = Builder.get_current()
456	    builder.add_operation_variadic(Continue, (), dict(values=()))
457	    op = builder.ops[-1]
458	    next_values = tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc) for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
459	    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, next_values))
460	
461	
462	# Maps to BreakOp
463	@dataclass(eq=False)
464	class Break(Operation, opcode="break", terminator=True):
465	    values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
466	
467	    @override
468	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
469	        output_values = [ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.values]
470	        bc.encode_BreakOp(ctx.builder, output_values)
471	        return ()
472	
473	    @override
474	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
475	        return f"break {', '.join([x.name for x in self.values])}"
476	
477	
478	def break_():
479	    scope = Scope.get_current()
480	    info = scope.loop_info
481	    assert info is not None
482	
483	    if info.flatten:
484	        return
485	
486	    for ctx_state in scope.context_stack[scope.loop_context_stack_depth:]:
487	        ctx_state.exit_callback()
488	
489	    builder = Builder.get_current()
490	    builder.add_operation_variadic(Break, (), dict(values=()))
491	    op = builder.ops[-1]
492	    outputs = tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc) for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
493	    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, outputs))
494	
495	
496	# Maps to YieldOp
497	@dataclass(eq=False)
498	class EndBranch(Operation, opcode="end_branch", terminator=True):
499	    outputs: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
500	
501	    @override
502	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
503	        output_values = tuple(ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.outputs)
504	        bc.encode_YieldOp(ctx.builder, output_values)
505	        return ()
506	
507	    @override
508	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
509	        return f"yield {', '.join([x.name for x in self.outputs])}"
510	
511	
512	def end_branch(output: Var | None):
513	    scope = Scope.get_current()
514	    info = scope.if_else_info
515	    outputs = () if output is None else (output,)
516	    if info.flatten:
517	        op = None
518	    else:
519	        builder = Builder.get_current()
520	        builder.add_operation_variadic(EndBranch, (), dict(outputs=()))
521	        op = builder.ops[-1]
522	        outputs += tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc)
523	                         for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
524	    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, outputs))
525	
526	
527	@dataclass(eq=False)
528	class Return(Operation, opcode="return", terminator=True):
529	
530	    @override
531	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
532	        bc.encode_ReturnOp(ctx.builder, ())
533	        return ()
534	
535	    @override
536	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
537	        return "return"
538	
539	
540	def return_(value: Var | None):
541	    if value is not None and value.get_type() is not NONE:
542	        raise TileTypeError("Tile kernels cannot return values")
543	    add_operation_variadic(Return, ())
544	
545	
546	@dataclass(eq=False)
547	class MakeDummy(Operation, opcode="make_dummy"):
548	    """Placeholder value inserted for undefined variable in loop.
549	
550	    The use case for undefined variables is to represent loop's
551	    initial_values or continue/break's next_values during type inference or
552	    post dead code elimination.
553	    """
554	
555	    @override
556	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
557	        ty = ctx.typeof(self.result_var)
558	        if isinstance(ty, TokenTy):
559	            return bc.encode_MakeTokenOp(ctx.builder, ctx.type_table.Token)
560	        from cuda.tile._ir.type import TileTy
561	        if isinstance(ty, TileTy) and is_pointer_dtype(ty.dtype):
562	            int_ty = TileTy(dtype=int64, shape=ty.shape)
563	            const = ctx.constant(0, int_ty)
564	            return bc.encode_IntToPtrOp(ctx.builder, typeid(ctx.type_table, ty), const)
565	        return ctx.constant(0, ty)
566	
567	
568	def _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(vars: Sequence[Var],
569	                                  actual_types: Sequence[Type]) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
570	    return tuple(add_operation(MakeDummy, actual)
571	                 if isinstance(v.get_type_allow_invalid(), InvalidType)
572	                 else v
573	                 for v, actual in zip(vars, actual_types, strict=True))
574

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2026> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Sequence

from typing_extensions import override

from cuda.tile._datatype import TileTypeError, int64, is_pointer_dtype
from cuda.tile._ir import hir_stubs, hir
from cuda.tile._ir.aggregate_support import flatten_block_parameters, expand_aggregate_var, \
    flatten_aggregate_types, flatten_aggregates, unflatten_aggregates
from cuda.tile._ir.core_ops import store_var, store_invalid
from cuda.tile._ir.ir import Operation, Var, operand, Block, nested_block, Builder, LoopVarState, \
    PhiState, ConstantState, enter_nested_block, add_operation_variadic, add_operation
from cuda.tile._ir.op_impl import ImplRegistry, require_optional_range_type, \
    require_bool_scalar_type
from cuda.tile._ir.scope import Scope, ControlFlowInfo, JumpInfo
from cuda.tile._ir.type import InvalidType, TupleValue, RangeValue, NONE, TokenTy, Type
from cuda.tile._ir2bytecode import BytecodeContext, typeid, generate_bytecode_for_block
import cuda.tile._bytecode as bc


_registry = ImplRegistry()
impl = _registry.impl


def control_flow_impl_registry() -> ImplRegistry:
    return _registry


@dataclass(eq=False)
class Loop(Operation, opcode="loop"):
    start: Var | None = operand()
    stop: Var | None = operand()
    step: Var | None = operand()
    initial_values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
    body: Block = nested_block()

    @property
    def is_for_loop(self) -> bool:
        return self.start is not None

    @property
    def induction_var(self):
        assert self.is_for_loop
        return self.nested_blocks[0].params[0]

    @property
    def body_vars(self) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
        return self.body.params[1:] if self.is_for_loop else self.body.params

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[bc.Value, ...]:
        types = tuple(x.get_type() for x in self.body_vars)
        initial_values = [ctx.get_value(input_var)
                          for input_var in self.initial_values]
        result_type_ids = [typeid(ctx.type_table, ty) for ty in types]

        if self.is_for_loop:
            start, stop, step = (ctx.get_value(x) for x in (self.start, self.stop, self.step))
            nested_builder = bc.encode_ForOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, start, stop, step,
                                             initial_values, unsignedCmp=False)
            induction_var_type_id = ctx.typeid_of(self.induction_var)
            block_arg_type_ids = (induction_var_type_id, *result_type_ids)
        else:
            nested_builder = bc.encode_LoopOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, initial_values)
            block_arg_type_ids = result_type_ids

        with nested_builder.new_block(block_arg_type_ids) as block_args, ctx.enter_loop(self):
            block_args = iter(block_args)
            if self.is_for_loop:
                ctx.set_value(self.induction_var, next(block_args))
            for var, value in zip(self.body_vars, block_args, strict=True):
                ctx.set_value(var, value)
            generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx, self.body)

        return nested_builder.done()

    @override
    def _to_string_block_prefixes(self) -> list[str]:
        return ["do"]

    @override
    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
        def format_var(var):
            ty = var.try_get_type()
            if ty is None:
                return var.name
            return f"{var.name}: {ty}"

        if self.is_for_loop:
            body_vars = self.body.params[1:]
            header_str = (f"for {self.body.params[0].name}"
                          f" in range({self.start.name}, {self.stop.name}, {self.step.name})")
        else:
            body_vars = self.body.params
            header_str = "loop"

        carried_vars_str = ", ".join(f"{format_var(b)} = {i.name}"
                                     for b, i in zip(body_vars, self.initial_values))
        return f"{header_str} (with {carried_vars_str})"


@impl(hir_stubs.loop)
async def loop_impl(body: hir.Block, iterable: Var):
    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block, retarget_loc

    scope = Scope.get_current()
    range_ty = require_optional_range_type(iterable)
    if range_ty is None and body.jump == hir.Jump.BREAK and not _have_nested_jump(body.calls):
        # In ast2hir, we create a loop around the function body in order to support early returns.
        # But if there is no early return, we can remove the loop. In this case, the loop will only
        # have a "break" at the end of the loop body, and no other break/continue statements.
        info = ControlFlowInfo((), flatten=True)
        with scope.change_loop_info(info):
            await dispatch_hir_block(body)
        return

    builder = Builder.get_current()
    stored_locals = tuple(sorted(body.stored_indices))
    var_states = tuple(LoopVarState(PhiState(initial_constant_state=ConstantState.NONCONSTANT),
                                    PhiState())
                       for _ in stored_locals)
    initial_values = tuple(scope.local.get(index, builder.loc) for index in stored_locals)
    body_params = []

    # Logic specific to `for` loops:
    if range_ty is not None:
        # A `for` loop may have 0 iterations, so initial values need to be propagated
        # to the loop's results.
        for initial_var, state in zip(initial_values, var_states, strict=True):
            state.result_phi.propagate(initial_var)
        # Create an induction variable
        induction_var = builder.ir_ctx.make_temp(builder.loc)
        induction_var.set_type(builder.ir_ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(range_ty.dtype, ()))
        scope.hir2ir_varmap[body.params[0].id] = induction_var
        body_params.append(induction_var)

    # Process the loop body
    loop_info = ControlFlowInfo(stored_locals)
    body_loc = retarget_loc(body.loc, scope)
    with enter_nested_block(body_loc) as new_body, scope.change_loop_info(loop_info), \
            scope.local.enter_branch():
        # Define body variables. Not all of them will eventually be kept,
        # so we don't set the block parameters yet.
        body_vars = []
        for local_idx, initial_var, state in zip(
                stored_locals, initial_values, var_states, strict=True):
            state.body_phi.propagate(initial_var, allow_loose_typing=False)
            body_var = scope.local.redefine(local_idx, state.body_phi.last_loc)
            body_var.set_type(state.body_phi.ty)
            body_vars.append(body_var)

        flat_body_vars = flatten_block_parameters(body_vars)

        # Dispatch the body (hir.Block) to populate the new_body (ir.Block) with Operations
        await dispatch_hir_block(body)

    # Propagate type information from Continue/Break to body/result phis
    for jump_info in loop_info.jumps:
        is_continue = isinstance(jump_info.jump_op, Continue)
        assert is_continue or isinstance(jump_info.jump_op, Break)
        for output, state in zip(jump_info.outputs, var_states, strict=True):
            if is_continue:
                state.body_phi.propagate(output, fail_eagerly=True)
            if range_ty is not None or not is_continue:
                state.result_phi.propagate(output)

    # Determine the final loop variable types and filter out invalid variables
    mask = []
    for i, (body_var, state) in enumerate(zip(body_vars, var_states, strict=True)):
        was_valid = not isinstance(body_var.get_type_allow_invalid(), InvalidType)
        state.finalize_loopvar_type(body_var)
        ty = body_var.get_type_allow_invalid()
        is_valid = not isinstance(ty, InvalidType)
        mask.append(is_valid)
        if not was_valid and is_valid and ty.is_aggregate():
            # The initial variable is invalid but the loop variable is preserved,
            # and the loop variable is aggregate. In this case, `flat_body_vars[i]`
            # will contain a single variable (previously of InvalidType,
            # and now of an aggregate type). Thus, we need to update it with
            # the according number of flattened undefined variables.
            assert len(flat_body_vars[i]) == 1
            undefined_items = expand_aggregate_var(body_var)
            flat_body_vars[i] = undefined_items
            # Create a fake aggregate value so that flatten_aggregates() doesn't fail
            # when we update the Continue/Break statements later.
            body_var.set_aggregate(TupleValue(undefined_items))

    # Set the block's parameters
    all_flattened_body_vars = sum((flattened for flattened, is_valid
                                   in zip(flat_body_vars, mask, strict=True) if is_valid), ())
    body_params.extend(all_flattened_body_vars)
    new_body.params = tuple(body_params)
    valid_var_types = tuple(v.get_type() for v, is_valid in zip(body_vars, mask, strict=True)
                            if is_valid)
    flat_var_types = flatten_aggregate_types(valid_var_types)

    # Update Continue/Break statements
    for jump_info in loop_info.jumps:
        values = tuple(out
                       for out, is_valid in zip(jump_info.outputs, mask, strict=True)
                       if is_valid)
        flat_values = flatten_aggregates(values, valid_var_types)
        # For undefined break/continue value, add a MakeDummy op as its producer
        flat_values = _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(flat_values, flat_var_types)
        assert len(flat_values) == len(all_flattened_body_vars)
        jump_info.jump_op.values = flat_values

    # Create the loop Operation
    valid_initial_values = tuple(v for v, is_valid
                                 in zip(initial_values, mask, strict=True)
                                 if is_valid)
    flat_initial_values = flatten_aggregates(valid_initial_values, valid_var_types)
    # For undefined initial value, add a MakeDummy op as its producer
    flat_initial_values = _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(flat_initial_values, flat_var_types)
    assert len(flat_initial_values) == len(all_flattened_body_vars)

    if range_ty is None:
        start = stop = step = None
    else:
        range_val = iterable.get_aggregate()
        assert isinstance(range_val, RangeValue)
        start, stop, step = range_val.start, range_val.stop, range_val.step
    flat_result_vars = add_operation_variadic(Loop, flat_var_types,
                                              start=start,
                                              stop=stop,
                                              step=step,
                                              initial_values=flat_initial_values,
                                              body=new_body)

    result_types = tuple(s.result_phi.ty for s, is_valid in zip(var_states, mask, strict=True)
                         if is_valid)
    result_vars = unflatten_aggregates(flat_result_vars, valid_var_types, result_types)

    # Finalize the scope & type information for valid result variables
    valid_var_states = tuple(s for s, is_valid in zip(var_states, mask, strict=True)
                             if is_valid)
    valid_stored_locals = tuple(local_idx
                                for local_idx, is_valid in zip(stored_locals, mask, strict=True)
                                if is_valid)
    for res, state, local_idx in zip(result_vars, valid_var_states, valid_stored_locals,
                                     strict=True):
        state.result_phi.finalize_constant_and_loose_type(res)
        store_var(local_idx, res, state.result_phi.last_loc)

    # For any names that are stored within the loop body but have an invalid result type,
    # we update the scope to point to an undefined variable of this invalid type, so that
    # using that variable afterwards would result in a type error.
    for body_var, state, local_idx, is_valid in zip(body_vars, var_states, stored_locals, mask,
                                                    strict=True):
        if not is_valid:
            store_invalid(local_idx, body_var.get_type_allow_invalid(),
                          state.result_phi.last_loc)

    # Do this check at the end because this may be an automatically inserted loop
    # around the helper function's body.
    if builder.block_restriction is not None:
        builder.block_restriction.validate_operation(Loop)


def _have_nested_jump(calls: Sequence[hir.Call]) -> bool:
    return any(
        block.jump != hir.Jump.END_BRANCH or _have_nested_jump(block.calls)
        for c in calls
        if c.callee is hir_stubs.if_else
        for block in c.args[1:]
    )


@dataclass(eq=False)
class IfElse(Operation, opcode="ifelse"):
    cond: Var = operand()
    then_block: Block = nested_block()
    else_block: Block = nested_block()

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[bc.Value, ...]:
        cond_val = ctx.get_value(self.cond)
        result_types = tuple(ctx.typeof(v) for v in self.result_vars)
        result_type_ids = tuple(typeid(ctx.type_table, t) for t in result_types)
        nested_builder = bc.encode_IfOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, cond_val)

        for block in (self.then_block, self.else_block):
            with nested_builder.new_block(()):
                generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx, block)

        return nested_builder.done()

    @override
    def _to_string_block_prefixes(self) -> list[str]:
        return ["then", "else"]

    @override
    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
        return f"if(cond={self.cond})"


@impl(hir_stubs.if_else)
async def if_else_impl(cond: Var, then_block: hir.Block, else_block: hir.Block) -> Var | None:
    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block, retarget_loc

    require_bool_scalar_type(cond)
    if cond.is_constant():
        branch_taken = then_block if cond.get_constant() else else_block
        return await _flatten_branch(branch_taken)

    builder = Builder.get_current()
    if builder.block_restriction is not None:
        builder.block_restriction.validate_operation(IfElse)

    # Get the total number of results by adding the number of stored variables.
    # Note: we sort the stored variable indices to make the order deterministic.
    scope = Scope.get_current()
    stored_locals = tuple(sorted(then_block.stored_indices | else_block.stored_indices))

    # Convert the "then" branch from HIR to IR
    info = ControlFlowInfo(stored_locals)
    then_loc = retarget_loc(then_block.loc, scope)
    with enter_nested_block(then_loc) as new_then_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
            scope.local.enter_branch():
        await dispatch_hir_block(then_block)

    # If "then" branch doesn't yield, transform our if-else into the following:
    #    if cond:
    #        <then_block>
    #    else:
    #        EndBranch
    #    <else_block>
    # This is to avoid the situation where none of the branches yield.
    else_loc = retarget_loc(else_block.loc, scope)
    if len(info.jumps) == 0:
        info = ControlFlowInfo(())
        with enter_nested_block(else_loc) as new_else_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
                scope.local.enter_branch():
            end_branch(None)
        add_operation_variadic(IfElse, (),
                               cond=cond, then_block=new_then_block, else_block=new_else_block)
        return await _flatten_branch(else_block)

    # Convert the "else" branch from HIR to IR
    with enter_nested_block(else_loc) as new_else_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
            scope.local.enter_branch():
        await dispatch_hir_block(else_block)

    # Do type/constant propagation
    num_results = len(info.jumps[0].outputs)
    result_phis = tuple(PhiState() for _ in range(num_results))
    for jump_info in info.jumps:
        for phi, v in zip(result_phis, jump_info.outputs, strict=True):
            phi.propagate(v)

    # Determine which results have valid types
    mask = tuple(not isinstance(phi.ty, InvalidType) for phi in result_phis)
    valid_result_types = tuple(phi.ty for phi, is_valid in zip(result_phis, mask) if is_valid)

    # Update the EndBranch operations by setting the outputs
    for jump_info in info.jumps:
        outputs = tuple(v for v, is_valid in zip(jump_info.outputs, mask, strict=True)
                        if is_valid)
        flat_outputs = flatten_aggregates(outputs, valid_result_types)
        jump_info.jump_op.outputs = flat_outputs

    # Generate an IfElse op
    flat_types = flatten_aggregate_types(valid_result_types)
    flat_results = add_operation_variadic(IfElse, flat_types, cond=cond,
                                          then_block=new_then_block, else_block=new_else_block)
    valid_results = unflatten_aggregates(flat_results, valid_result_types, valid_result_types)

    # Finalize the constant/loose type information
    valid_result_phis = tuple(phi for phi, is_valid in zip(result_phis, mask) if is_valid)
    for var, phi in zip(valid_results, valid_result_phis, strict=True):
        phi.finalize_constant_and_loose_type(var)

    it = iter(valid_results)
    all_results = tuple(next(it) if is_valid else None for is_valid in mask)
    assert next(it, None) is None

    # Get/create variables for the explicit result
    num_explicit_results = num_results - len(stored_locals)
    if num_explicit_results == 0:
        ret = None
    else:
        assert num_explicit_results == 1
        ret = all_results[0]
        if ret is None:
            assert isinstance(result_phis[0].ty, InvalidType)
            ret = builder.ir_ctx.make_temp(builder.loc)
            ret.set_type(result_phis[0].ty)

    # Update the scope for stored named
    for res_var, phi, local_idx in zip(all_results[num_explicit_results:],
                                       result_phis[num_explicit_results:],
                                       stored_locals, strict=True):
        if res_var is None:
            store_invalid(local_idx, phi.ty, phi.last_loc)
        else:
            store_var(local_idx, res_var, phi.last_loc)

    return ret


@impl(hir_stubs.tuple_comp_if)
async def tuple_comp_if_impl(cond: Var, then_block: hir.Block) -> None:
    require_bool_scalar_type(cond)
    if not cond.is_constant():
        raise TileTypeError("Tuple comprehension if-conditions must be statically known;"
                            " if the condition can be evaluated at compile time,"
                            " wrap it with ct.static_eval()")
    if cond.get_constant():
        await _flatten_branch(then_block)


async def _flatten_branch(branch: hir.Block) -> Var | None:
    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block
    info = ControlFlowInfo((), flatten=True)
    with Scope.get_current().change_if_else_info(info):
        await dispatch_hir_block(branch)
    if len(info.jumps) == 0:
        return None
    else:
        assert len(info.jumps) == 1
        jump = info.jumps[0]
        assert len(jump.outputs) in (0, 1)
        return None if len(jump.outputs) == 0 else jump.outputs[0]


# Maps to ContinueOp in TileIR
@dataclass(eq=False)
class Continue(Operation, opcode="continue", terminator=True):
    values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
        next_values = [ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.values]
        bc.encode_ContinueOp(ctx.builder, next_values)
        return ()

    @override
    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
        return f"continue {', '.join([x.name for x in self.values])}"


def continue_():
    scope = Scope.get_current()
    info = scope.loop_info
    assert info is not None
    assert not info.flatten

    for ctx_state in scope.context_stack[scope.loop_context_stack_depth:]:
        ctx_state.exit_callback()

    builder = Builder.get_current()
    builder.add_operation_variadic(Continue, (), dict(values=()))
    op = builder.ops[-1]
    next_values = tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc) for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, next_values))


# Maps to BreakOp
@dataclass(eq=False)
class Break(Operation, opcode="break", terminator=True):
    values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
        output_values = [ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.values]
        bc.encode_BreakOp(ctx.builder, output_values)
        return ()

    @override
    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
        return f"break {', '.join([x.name for x in self.values])}"


def break_():
    scope = Scope.get_current()
    info = scope.loop_info
    assert info is not None

    if info.flatten:
        return

    for ctx_state in scope.context_stack[scope.loop_context_stack_depth:]:
        ctx_state.exit_callback()

    builder = Builder.get_current()
    builder.add_operation_variadic(Break, (), dict(values=()))
    op = builder.ops[-1]
    outputs = tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc) for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, outputs))


# Maps to YieldOp
@dataclass(eq=False)
class EndBranch(Operation, opcode="end_branch", terminator=True):
    outputs: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
        output_values = tuple(ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.outputs)
        bc.encode_YieldOp(ctx.builder, output_values)
        return ()

    @override
    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
        return f"yield {', '.join([x.name for x in self.outputs])}"


def end_branch(output: Var | None):
    scope = Scope.get_current()
    info = scope.if_else_info
    outputs = () if output is None else (output,)
    if info.flatten:
        op = None
    else:
        builder = Builder.get_current()
        builder.add_operation_variadic(EndBranch, (), dict(outputs=()))
        op = builder.ops[-1]
        outputs += tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc)
                         for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, outputs))


@dataclass(eq=False)
class Return(Operation, opcode="return", terminator=True):

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
        bc.encode_ReturnOp(ctx.builder, ())
        return ()

    @override
    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
        return "return"


def return_(value: Var | None):
    if value is not None and value.get_type() is not NONE:
        raise TileTypeError("Tile kernels cannot return values")
    add_operation_variadic(Return, ())


@dataclass(eq=False)
class MakeDummy(Operation, opcode="make_dummy"):
    """Placeholder value inserted for undefined variable in loop.

    The use case for undefined variables is to represent loop's
    initial_values or continue/break's next_values during type inference or
    post dead code elimination.
    """

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
        ty = ctx.typeof(self.result_var)
        if isinstance(ty, TokenTy):
            return bc.encode_MakeTokenOp(ctx.builder, ctx.type_table.Token)
        from cuda.tile._ir.type import TileTy
        if isinstance(ty, TileTy) and is_pointer_dtype(ty.dtype):
            int_ty = TileTy(dtype=int64, shape=ty.shape)
            const = ctx.constant(0, int_ty)
            return bc.encode_IntToPtrOp(ctx.builder, typeid(ctx.type_table, ty), const)
        return ctx.constant(0, ty)


def _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(vars: Sequence[Var],
                                  actual_types: Sequence[Type]) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
    return tuple(add_operation(MakeDummy, actual)
                 if isinstance(v.get_type_allow_invalid(), InvalidType)
                 else v
                 for v, actual in zip(vars, actual_types, strict=True))

def encode_EntryOp(
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    sym_name: str,
    function_type: TypeId,
    arg_attrs: Optional[Sequence[TaggedAttribute]],
    res_attrs: Optional[Sequence[TaggedAttribute]],
    optimization_hints: Optional[OptimizationHints],
) -> NestedBlockBuilder:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(22, _buf)
    # Flags
    encode_varint((arg_attrs is not None)
                  | ((res_attrs is not None) << 1)
                  | ((optimization_hints is not None) << 2), _buf)
    # Attributes
    code_builder.encode_opattr_str(sym_name)
    code_builder.encode_opattr_typeid(function_type)
    if arg_attrs is not None:
        code_builder.encode_opattr_array(arg_attrs)
    if res_attrs is not None:
        code_builder.encode_opattr_array(res_attrs)
    if optimization_hints is not None:
        code_builder.encode_opattr_optimization_hints(optimization_hints)
    return code_builder.new_op_with_nested_blocks(0, 1)


def encode_Exp2Op(
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    result_type: TypeId,
    source: Value,
    flush_to_zero: bool,
) -> Value:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(24, _buf)
    # Result types
    encode_typeid(result_type, _buf)
    # Flags
    encode_varint(bool(flush_to_zero), _buf)
    # Operands
    encode_operand(source, _buf)
    return code_builder.new_op()


def encode_ExpOp(
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    result_type: TypeId,
    source: Value,
    rounding_mode: RoundingMode,  # since 13.3
) -> Value:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(23, _buf)
    # Result types
    encode_typeid(result_type, _buf)
    # Attributes
    if code_builder.version >= BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
        code_builder.encode_opattr_enum(RoundingMode, rounding_mode)
    else:
        assert rounding_mode == RoundingMode.FULL
    # Operands
    encode_operand(source, _buf)
    return code_builder.new_op()


def encode_ExtIOp(
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    to_type: TypeId,
    from_: Value,
    signedness: Signedness,
) -> Value:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(37, _buf)
    # Result types
    encode_typeid(to_type, _buf)
    # Attributes
    code_builder.encode_opattr_enum(Signedness, signedness)
    # Operands
    encode_operand(from_, _buf)
    return code_builder.new_op()

def encode_ForOp(
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    result_types: Sequence[TypeId],
    lowerBound: Value,
    upperBound: Value,
    step: Value,
    initValues: Sequence[Value],
    unsignedCmp: bool,  # since 13.2
) -> NestedBlockBuilder:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(41, _buf)
    # Variadic result types
    encode_sized_typeid_seq(result_types, _buf)
    # Flags
    _flag_bits = bool(unsignedCmp)
    assert _flag_bits < 1 or code_builder.version >= BytecodeVersion.V_13_2
    if code_builder.version >= BytecodeVersion.V_13_2:
        encode_varint(_flag_bits, _buf)
    # Operands
    encode_varint(3 + len(initValues), _buf)
    encode_operand(lowerBound, _buf)
    encode_operand(upperBound, _buf)
    encode_operand(step, _buf)
    encode_unsized_variadic_operands(initValues, _buf)
    return code_builder.new_op_with_nested_blocks(len(result_types), 1)


def encode_GdcLaunchDependentsTkoOp(  # since 13.4
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    result_token_type: TypeId,  # since 13.4
    token: Optional[Value],  # since 13.4
) -> Value:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(119, _buf)
    # Result types
    encode_typeid(result_token_type, _buf)
    # Operands
    encode_varint(int(token is not None), _buf)
    encode_optional_operand(token, _buf)
    return code_builder.new_op()


def encode_GdcWaitTkoOp(  # since 13.4
    code_builder: CodeBuilder,
    result_token_type: TypeId,  # since 13.4
    token: Optional[Value],  # since 13.4
) -> Value:
    _buf = code_builder.buf
    # Opcode
    encode_varint(120, _buf)
    # Result types
    encode_typeid(result_token_type, _buf)
    # Operands
    encode_varint(int(token is not None), _buf)
    encode_optional_operand(token, _buf)
    return code_builder.new_op()

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

import enum
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Sequence, Mapping, Any, Iterator, Tuple, NamedTuple

from .attribute import EntryHints, make_entry_hints
from .basic import encode_varint, StringTable, Table
from .code_builder import CodeBuilder, Value
from .constant import ConstantTable
from .debug_info import DebugAttrId, DebugAttrTable
from .encodings import SymbolVisibility
from .type import TypeTable, TypeId, encode_typeid
from .version import BytecodeVersion


class FunctionBuilder(NamedTuple):
    code_builder: CodeBuilder
    parameters: Tuple[Value, ...]


class GlobalSection:
    def __init__(self, string_table: StringTable, constant_table: ConstantTable,
                 version: BytecodeVersion):
        self._string_table = string_table
        self._constant_table = constant_table
        self._version = version
        self._defined_names = set()
        self._contents = bytearray()

    def define_global(self, name: str, type: TypeId, data: bytes, alignment: int = 0,
                      constant: bool = False,
                      symbol_visibility: SymbolVisibility = SymbolVisibility.Public):
        if name in self._defined_names:
            raise ValueError(f"Global `{name}` has already been defined")
        name_id = self._string_table[name.encode()]
        encode_varint(name_id.string_id, self._contents)
        encode_typeid(type, self._contents)
        encode_varint(self._constant_table.dense_constant(data).constant_id, self._contents)
        encode_varint(alignment, self._contents)
        if self._version >= BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
            self._contents.extend(symbol_visibility._value_)
            encode_varint(int(constant), self._contents)
        self._defined_names.add(name)

    def is_defined(self, global_name: str):
        return global_name in self._defined_names


class BytecodeWriter:
    def __init__(self, buf: bytearray, version: BytecodeVersion):
        self._num_functions = 0
        self.debug_info = []
        self._buf = buf
        self._string_table = StringTable()
        self._debug_attr_table = DebugAttrTable(self._string_table)
        self._constant_table = ConstantTable()
        self._type_table = TypeTable(version)
        self._global_section = GlobalSection(self._string_table, self._constant_table, version)
        self.version = version

    @property
    def debug_attr_table(self) -> DebugAttrTable:
        return self._debug_attr_table

    @property
    def constant_table(self) -> ConstantTable:
        return self._constant_table

    @property
    def type_table(self) -> TypeTable:
        return self._type_table

    @property
    def global_section(self) -> GlobalSection:
        return self._global_section

    @contextmanager
    def function(self,
                 name: str,
                 parameter_types: Sequence[TypeId],
                 result_types: Sequence[TypeId],
                 entry_point: bool,
                 hints: Mapping[str, EntryHints],
                 debug_attr: DebugAttrId) -> Iterator[FunctionBuilder]:
        self._num_functions += 1
        encode_varint(self._string_table[name.encode()].string_id, self._buf)
        sig_ty = self._type_table.function(parameter_types, result_types)
        encode_typeid(sig_ty, self._buf)
        self._buf.append((0x02 | (0x04 if hints else 0)) if entry_point else 0)
        self.debug_info.append([debug_attr])
        encode_varint(len(self.debug_info), self._buf)

        if entry_point and hints:
            make_entry_hints(hints).encode_tagged(self._string_table, self._buf)

        builder = CodeBuilder(buf=bytearray(),
                              version=self.version,
                              string_table=self._string_table,
                              constant_table=self._constant_table,
                              debug_attr_per_op=self.debug_info[-1])
        yield FunctionBuilder(builder, builder._make_value_tuple(len(parameter_types)))
        encode_varint(len(builder.buf), self._buf)
        self._buf.extend(builder.buf)


@contextmanager
def write_bytecode(num_functions: int, buf: bytearray,
                   version: BytecodeVersion) -> Iterator[BytecodeWriter]:
    _write_header(buf, version)

    with _section(_Section.Func, 8, buf) as section_buf:
        encode_varint(num_functions, section_buf)
        w = BytecodeWriter(section_buf, version)
        yield w
        assert w._num_functions == num_functions

    _write_global_section(w.global_section, buf)

    with _section(_Section.Constant, 8, buf) as section_buf:
        _write_table(w.constant_table, 8, section_buf)

    _write_debug_info_section(w.debug_info, w.debug_attr_table, buf)

    with _section(_Section.Type, 4, buf) as section_buf:
        _write_table(w.type_table, 4, section_buf)

    with _section(_Section.String, 4, buf) as section_buf:
